mission.title: flagger: Keptn Metrics Provider
mission.description: Keptn Metrics Provider. Requested by 5+ users.

steps.title: Check current flagger deployment
steps.description:
Verify your flagger version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
helm list -n flagger 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working flagger installation.

steps.title: Review flagger configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant flagger configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
kubectl get configmap -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=flagger
```
## Describe the feature

I would like to suggest the addition of a [Keptn](https://keptn.sh) metrics provider to the list of already implemented providers.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Keptn Metrics Provider
steps.description:
Hi  I'm happy to have this integration in Flagger. I see that the API version is at `v1beta1`, would you be able to help maintain the integration in Flagger going forward?

See the source issue for community-verified solutions.

steps.title: Upgrade flagger to include the fix
steps.description:
If the fix is included in a newer release, upgrade flagger:
```bash
helm repo update
helm upgrade flagger flagger/flagger --namespace flagger
```
Verify the upgrade:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flagger
helm list -n flagger
```

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n flagger -l app.kubernetes.io/name=flagger
kubectl get events -n flagger --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Keptn Metrics Provider" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary: Hi  I'm happy to have this integration in Flagger. I see that the API version is at `v1beta1`, would you be able to help maintain the integration in Flagger going forward?

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with flagger installed or the issue environment reproducible.
